content_en: .r-.JJ / . Y J» . ; r-t-f" J. i.S.J / r '-.1,Jఢ "Do not extol me like the Christians have [wrongfully] extolled the son of Mary. Verily, I am but His [Allah's] servant. Therefore, say, 'The servant of Allah and His Messenger."' Then Al-Nawawi describes the Prophet (peace be upon him) with two very special characteristics. The Prophet (peace be upon him) is both the habeeb ("the loved one") and the khaleel ("most dear friend") of Allah. There is a difference of opinion concerning which one of these two characteristics is the most praiseworthy position. Understanding the differences between the two concepts leads to the resolution of this dispute. Ibn Abu al-Izz in his famous commentary to the Creed of al-Tahaawi has discussed the concepts of khullah2 and muhabbah, 1 Some Sufis go so far as to deny the Prophet's human status whatsoever. Some even claim that the Prophet (peace be upon him) was all light and, therefore, he cast no shadow at all. Hence, the title of a biography of the Prophet (peace be upon him), Muhammad: The Shadowless Prophet. 2 Khullah, meaning "close friendship", comes from the same root as khaleel, meaning "close, dear friend". Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "If I were to take anyone from among the people of the earth for a khaleel, I would have taken Abu Bakr as a khaleel. But your companion (that is, the Prophet himself) is the khaleel of Allah."1 Another version states, "I have no relationship to any khaleel [of this world]. If I were to take a khaleel from the inhabitants of the earth, I would have taken Abu Bakr as a khaleel." In yet another version, the words are, "Allah has taken me as a khaleel as He had taken Abraham as a khaleel."2 The Prophet (peace be upon him) made it clear that it was not proper for him to have a khaleel from the humans. But if that were possible, the person most deserving to be his khaleel would have been Abu Bakr. At the same time, though, on different occasions, he stated that he loved certain people. For example, he said to Muadh, "By Allah, I love you."3 He made a similar statement to the Ansar of Madinah. Zaid ibn Harithah and his son Usamah were known to be beloved to the Prophet (peace be upon him). There are other examples of this nature. 'Amr ibn al-As once asked the Prophet, "What person is most beloved to you?" He answered, " Aishah." He said, "From among the men?" He answered, "Her father."4 It is clear from these hadith that khullah is a special kind of love. Its object is loved due to its own intrinsic value and not for any other external reason. Obviously, that which is loved for some external reason falls short of that which is loved solely for itself. Since khullah is the most perfect love and permeates the whole being of the lover, it cannot have more than one object. That is why when Allah took Abraham as a khaleel, and the latter prayed for a son, and Allah gave him Ishmael, and Ishmael began to attract the love of Abraham, Allah disliked that someone else should take the place He had solely in his heart. He therefore tested Abraham. He asked him to sacrifice his son so that his khullah would be established over his love for his son. Abraham submitted to Allah and was ready to carry out His command. When he intended to slaughter his son, his khullah for Allah was proven, and its supremacy over the love for his son was established. At that moment, Allah repealed His command, and Abraham offered a great animal for sacrifice as a ransom for the son. What Allah had required from Abraham was absolute submission to His 1 Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim. 2 Recorded by Muslim. 3 Recorded by Abu Dawood, Ahmad, al-Nasaai, ibn Hibbaan, and al-Haakim According to Shuaib al-Arnaoot, its chain is sahih. See al-Arnaut's footnotes to Ali ibn Abu al-Izz, Sharh al­ Aqeedah al-Tahawiya (Beirut: Muassasat al-Risaalah, 1988), vol. 2, p. 397. 4 Recorded by Al-Bukhari and Muslim. Commentary on Al-Nawawi's Introduction command. When this was proved, killing the son ceased to be something good, consequently that command was withdrawn. Since then, animal sacrifice was instituted for his followers till the end of the world. 1 Some people think that the Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him) was the only khaleel of Allah. That is not true. This characteristic is also true for the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). Again, ibn Abu al-Izz wrote, It is confirmed that the Prophet (peace be on him) enjoyed the highest degree of Allah's love, which is khullah (closeness). He said, "Allah took me as His most intimate friend (khaleelJ as He had earlier taken Abraham as His most intimate friend." On another occasion he said, "If I were to take an inhabitant of the Earth as my most intimate friend, I would choose Abu Bakr.
content_id:
Al-Nawawi kemudian menyifati Nabi Muhammad dengan dua sifat yang sangat istimewa: ia adalah habeeb (yang dicintai) dan khaleel (sahabat karib yang paling dekat) Allah. Terdapat perbedaan pendapat tentang mana di antara keduanya yang lebih tinggi kedudukannya. Pemahaman tentang perbedaan antara dua konsep ini akan menyelesaikan perselisihan tersebut. Ibn Abi al-Izz dalam syarh-nya yang terkenal atas Aqidah al-Thahawiyyah membahas konsep khullah (persahabatan karib) dan mahabbah (cinta).

Nabi bersabda: 'Seandainya aku mengambil seseorang dari penduduk bumi sebagai khaleel, niscaya aku akan mengambil Abu Bakr sebagai khaleel. Namun sahabat kalian ini (yakni Nabi sendiri) adalah khaleel Allah.' Riwayat lain menyebutkan: 'Aku tidak memiliki hubungan khaleel dengan siapa pun. Seandainya aku mengambil seorang khaleel dari penduduk bumi, aku akan memilih Abu Bakr.' Dan dalam riwayat lain: 'Allah telah mengambilku sebagai khaleel sebagaimana Ia telah mengambil Ibrahim sebagai khaleel.' Nabi memperjelas bahwa tidak pantas baginya memiliki khaleel dari kalangan manusia. Namun jika itu mungkin, maka orang yang paling berhak menjadi khaleel-nya adalah Abu Bakr.

Pada saat yang sama, dalam berbagai kesempatan Nabi juga menyatakan bahwa ia mencintai orang-orang tertentu. Misalnya kepada Mu'adz ia berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku mencintaimu.' Zaid ibn Haritsah dan putranya Usamah dikenal sebagai orang-orang yang dicintai Nabi. Dari hadis-hadis ini jelas bahwa khullah adalah jenis cinta yang sangat istimewa — objeknya dicintai karena nilai intrinsiknya sendiri, bukan karena alasan eksternal apa pun. Karena khullah adalah cinta yang paling sempurna dan meresap ke seluruh keberadaan sang pecinta, ia tidak bisa memiliki lebih dari satu objek. Itulah mengapa ketika Allah mengambil Ibrahim sebagai khaleel dan Ismail mulai menarik kecintaan Ibrahim, Allah menguji Ibrahim dengan memerintahkannya menyembelih putranya — agar khullah-nya kepada Allah terbukti lebih kuat dari kecintaannya kepada putranya. Ketika Ibrahim bersiap melaksanakan perintah itu, khullah-nya kepada Allah terbukti. Allah pun mencabut perintah itu, dan Ibrahim menyembelih seekor hewan besar sebagai tebusan.

Sebagian orang mengira bahwa hanya Ibrahim yang menjadi khaleel Allah, namun ini tidak benar. Kedudukan ini juga berlaku bagi Nabi Muhammad. Ibn Abi al-Izz menulis: 'Telah dikonfirmasi bahwa Nabi menikmati derajat tertinggi cinta Allah, yaitu khullah. Ia bersabda: Allah mengambilku sebagai khaleel sebagaimana Ia telah mengambil Ibrahim sebagai khaleel.' Dengan demikian, khullah bukan hanya milik Ibrahim, melainkan juga milik Nabi Muhammad.
content_ar:
يُعالج هذا الجزء مسألة دقيقة في وصف النبي ﷺ: هل هو حبيب الله أم خليله؟ وقد وصفه الإمام النووي بالوصفين معاً في مقدمة أربعينه. والفرق بين المحبة والخُلَّة أن الخُلَّة — مشتقة من «الخليل» — هي المحبة التي تتخلّل أعماق القلب وتملأ خِلاله، وهي أعلى درجات المحبة وأرسخها. ولذلك يرى بعض العلماء أن الخُلَّة أعلى مقاماً من مجرد المحبة. وقد ذهب آخرون إلى أن المحبة هي الأعلى. وللشراح والعلماء في هذه المسألة خلاف ثري.

والصواب الذي ذهب إليه ابن أبي العز الحنفي في شرحه على الطحاوية وهو ما يعرضه الشارح هنا: أن النبي ﷺ جمع له الوصفان معاً. فقد ثبت في الصحيحين قوله ﷺ: «إن الله اتخذني خليلاً كما اتخذ إبراهيم خليلاً». فالخُلَّة ثابتة له كما ثبتت لإبراهيم عليه السلام. وكذلك المحبة التي جاءت مؤكدة في نصوص كثيرة. فالنبي ﷺ إذن خليل الله وحبيبه في آنٍ واحد، وذلك مقام جمع له ما لم يُجمع لسواه.

وقد نبّه الشارح إلى خطأ من يقول: إن الخُلَّة كانت لإبراهيم دون محمد ﷺ، وإن محمداً ﷺ إنما أُعطي المحبة دون الخُلَّة. وهذا قول مردود بالنص الصريح. وفي هذا الباب فائدة عقدية وتربوية: أن التعظيم الصحيح للنبي ﷺ يكون بالإيمان بما أثبته الله له وبالاقتداء بسنته والتقيد بحدوده، لا بالإطراء الغالي الذي نهى عنه ﷺ بقوله: «لا تُطروني كما أطرت النصارى ابن مريم».
